design_philosophy.core_loop: Build and manage an amusement park by constructing rides/shops, hiring staff, managing finances, and optimizing visitor satisfaction to increase park value and progress to new locations worldwide
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing capital investment in attractions and staff against operational costs and visitor demand; managing maintenance failures and park reputation against profit margins
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Tycoon fantasy of designing and operating a successful amusement park empire, expanding from modest beginnings to a global chain of theme parks
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game emphasizes realism in park management simulation with emergent problems (staff strikes, vandalism, ride explosions from neglect) that create organic challenges. Refuses to simplify the business loop—negotiation failures have real consequences, and logistics complexity scales with difficulty level. Rewards long-term thinking and staff management over arcade reflexes.

difficulty.checkpointing: Calendar-based progression with annual performance reviews; can save parks at any time
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three explicit difficulty levels with player ability to switch modes at any time. Higher difficulties add logistics simulation (research management, stock/shares, price negotiations). Sandbox mode removes management aspects entirely. Game speed adjustable by player.

progression.upgrade_system: Research system unlocks new rides, shops, facilities, and staff efficiency improvements. Research funding allocation determined by player. Higher ride durability and larger bus capacity available through research.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression via park auction/sale. Once sufficient money accumulated, player auctions current park and purchases new plot in different world location with distinct environmental factors (economy, weather, terrain, land value). Unlocks themed settings (desert, glacier, etc. on console versions).
replayability_hooks: Different world locations with varying economic and environmental conditions affect gameplay strategy
Multiple difficulty levels encourage replays with different management challenges
Competitive aspect: annual performance judged against rivals
Achievement hunting: trophies for accomplishments like longest roller coaster
Customizable shop attributes create emergent outcomes based on player decisions

world_building_techniques: Environmental progression through themed park locations (UK starting location expands globally with distinct geographical settings)
Implicit world-building through economic simulation (different economies, land values, weather patterns by region)
Thematic ride/shop variety (40+ attractions including clowns, medieval shows) suggests cultural diversity

faction_indicators: Staff unions (wages/price negotiations can result in strikes)
Rival park operators (annual competitive performance reviews)
Visitor demographics (customizable attractions affect different customer behaviors)
danger_signals: Vandalism events triggered by visitor unhappiness (thugs pop balloons, steal food, beat entertainers)
Ride explosions from deferred maintenance creating cascading park failures
Staff shortages leading to messy conditions, crime, and visitor attrition
Failed negotiations causing supply loss or labor strikes
environmental_storytelling: Messy footpaths indicate insufficient janitorial staff
Visitor clustering around certain attractions reveals preference patterns
Thematic scenery purchases (trees, fountains) communicate park ambition and prestige
